citation:ixbrl.name: iXBRL (XBRL International)
citation:ixbrl.title: iXBRL
citation:ixbrl.evidence.0.sourceQuote: iXBRL, or Inline XBRL, is an open standard that enables a single document to provide both human-readable and structured, machine-readable data.
citation:ixbrl.evidence.0.note: The source describes inline human-readable plus machine-readable structure; the fact store normalizes that to inlineSupport = yes.
citation:factscore.name: FActScore (Min et al., 2023)
citation:factscore.title: FActScore: Fine-grained Atomic Evaluation of Factual Precision in Long Form Text Generation
citation:factscore.evidence.0.sourceQuot: we also introduce an automated model that estimates FACTSCORE using retrieval and a strong language model
citation:factscore.evidence.0.note: The stored claim compresses the quoted description into a shorter verification-mode label.
citation:safe.name: SAFE (Wei et al., 2024)
citation:safe.title: Long-form factuality in large language models
citation:safe.evidence.0.sourceQuote: SAFE utilizes an LLM to break down a long-form response into a set of individual facts and to evaluate the accuracy of each fact using a multi-step reasoning process comprising sending search queries to Google Search and determining whether a fact is supported by the search results.
citation:safe.evidence.0.note: The fact-store value condenses the exact quoted mechanism to the authority source it checks against.
citation:factool.name: FacTool (Chern et al., 2023)
citation:factool.title: FacTool: Factuality Detection in Generative AI -- A Tool Augmented Framework for Multi-Task and Multi-Domain Scenarios
citation:factool.evidence.0.sourceQuote: we propose FacTool, a task and domain agnostic framework for detecting factual errors of texts generated by large language models
citation:factool.evidence.0.note: The source quote motivates the category, but the category label itself is our comparative abstraction.
citation:guardrails-ai.name: Guardrails AI
citation:guardrails-ai.title: Validators
citation:guardrails-ai.evidence.0.source: Validators are how we apply quality controls to the outputs of LLMs.
citation:guardrails-ai.evidence.0.note: The comparative category is derived directly from the docs’ description of validators as output quality controls.
citation:dspy-assertions.name: DSPy Assertions (Singhvi et al., 2023)
citation:dspy-assertions.title: DSPy Assertions: Computational Constraints for Self-Refining Language Model Pipelines
citation:dspy-assertions.evidence.0.sour: We also propose strategies to use assertions at inference time for automatic self-refinement with LMs.
citation:dspy-assertions.evidence.0.note: The stored verification-mode label summarizes the quoted self-refinement mechanism and the paper’s broader constraint framing.
citation:nemo-guardrails.name: NeMo Guardrails (NVIDIA)
citation:nemo-guardrails.title: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails
citation:nemo-guardrails.evidence.0.sour: NeMo Guardrails is an open-source toolkit for easily adding programmable guardrails to LLM-based conversational systems.
citation:nemo-guardrails.evidence.0.note: The stored mode keeps the exact ‘programmable guardrails’ phrase and expands it into the comparative terminology used on this page.
citation:rarr.name: RARR (Gao et al., 2022)
citation:rarr.title: RARR: Researching and Revising What Language Models Say, Using Language Models
citation:rarr.evidence.0.sourceQuote: we propose RARR (Retrofit Attribution using Research and Revision), a system that 1) automatically finds attribution for the output of any text generation model and 2) post-edits the output to fix unsupported content
citation:rarr.evidence.0.note: The stored category is a short label that directly compresses the two capabilities named in the quoted sentence.
citation:webgpt.name: WebGPT (Nakano et al., 2021)
citation:webgpt.title: WebGPT: Improving the factual accuracy of language models through web browsing
citation:webgpt.evidence.0.sourceQuote: We fine-tune GPT-3 to answer long-form questions using a text-based web-browsing environment, which allows the model to search and navigate the web.
citation:webgpt.evidence.0.note: The fact-store value abstracts the browsing environment to the authority layer it relies on: web pages.
citation:gemini-double-check.name: Gemini double-check (Google)
citation:gemini-double-check.title: View related sources & double-check responses from Gemini Apps
When sources are available, you can find the Sources button at the bottom of the response or in-line throughout the response.
The source explicitly says sources may appear inline or below the response; the fact store normalizes that to partial inline support.
